source: clang-tidy, cppcheck, CERT C Coding Standard

rules.name: malloc-without-free
rules.message: malloc() call — ensure corresponding free() exists to prevent memory leaks.
rules.fix_hint: Verify every malloc has a matching free on all code paths, including error paths.

rules.name: buffer-overflow-risk
rules.message: Unsafe function with buffer overflow risk — use bounds-checked alternative.
rules.fix_hint: Replace gets→fgets, sprintf→snprintf, strcpy→strncpy or strlcpy, strcat→strncat or strlcat.

rules.name: hardcoded-credentials
rules.fix_hint: Move secrets to environment variables or a secure configuration file; load via getenv().

rules.name: console-output
rules.message: printf() in production code — use a logging framework instead.
rules.fix_hint: Replace with syslog(), a project-specific logger, or guard with a debug/verbose flag.

rules.name: deep-nesting
rules.message: Deep nesting detected (4+ indent levels) — reduces readability.
rules.fix_hint: Extract inner logic into named functions or use early returns / goto cleanup pattern.

file_size._note: Lines of code. C files tend to be longer due to explicit memory management and boilerplate.

function_size._note: Detected via brace-counting heuristic, not grep.
